records.ety: [OE. phisike, fisike, OF. phisique, F. physique knowledge of nature, physics, L. physica, physice, fr. Gr. fysikh`, fr. fysiko`s natural, from fy`sis nature, fr. fy`ein to produce, grow, akin to E. be. See Be, and cf. Physics, Physique.]

defs: The art of healing diseases; the science of medicine; the theory or practice of medicine; -- an archaic term, superseded by medicine.
A specific internal application for the cure or relief of sickness; a remedy for disease; a medicine.
Specifically, a medicine that purges; a cathartic.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Physiced (fz"kt); p. pr. & vb. n. Physicking (fz"k*ng).]

defs: To treat with physic or medicine; to administer medicine to, esp. a cathartic; to operate on as a cathartic; to purge.
To work on as a remedy; to heal; to cure.
quotes: The labor we delight in physics pain.
A mind diseased no remedy can physic.
